methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
MightyFly, a Series A-stage autonomous drone company founded by Manal Habib, operates within the Drones & Autonomous sector. The broader comp universe consists of 8 private peers including Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation; no public comparables exist in this segment. MightyFly has raised $10M to date.

Valuation analysis relies on the Stage-Progression method, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $10M raised proxy, implying a $20M valuation. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting density thresholds—the four same-sector peers identified did not yield three or more datapoints in narrow filters, making sector-wide medians unreliable for this stage and tier.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median of $20M. This range reflects the single applicable method and inherent uncertainty around stage-progression multipliers in a nascent, heterogeneous sector. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer valuation data, variation in capital efficiency and go-to-market timing across the Drones & Autonomous landscape, and the limited precedent for Series A → Series B step-ups in early-stage autonomous vehicle subsectors. Investors should weight these constraints when assessing MightyFly's positioning relative to peers.
